micro_segments.title: Contradiction in Pesukim: 20 Amot vs. 30 Amot for the Dvir
Resolution: The 20 Amot Measured from the Top of the Keruvim Upward—Theological Significance of Non-Measurement
Principle of Masoret: Aron and Keruvim Do Not Take Up Physical Space—Implications for Divine Presence

macro_segments.title: The Keruvim's Physical Configuration: Multiple Explanations for Accommodating Wing Measurements

micro_segments.title: Rav Chana in the Name of Shmuel: Keruvim Stand via Miracle (Benes)
Challenge of Abaye: Alternative Explanation—Wings Like a Chicken, Body Positioned Below
Challenge of Rava: Wings Do Not Touch in the Middle—Side-by-Side Arrangement Without Meeting
Challenge of Rav Achai bar Yaakov: Diagonal Standing Position
Challenge of Rav Huna bar Rav Yehoshua: Expanding Airspace Above—Walls Taper Upward
Challenge of Rav Papa: Wings Bent Inward—Non-Straight Configuration
Challenge of Rav Ashi: Wings Crossed or Overlapped—Positioned on Different Planes

macro_segments.title: Orientation of the Keruvim: Dispute Between Rabbi Yochanan and Rabbi Elazar on Facing Direction

micro_segments.title: Two Views: Facing Each Other vs. Facing Toward the Bayit (Parochet)
Resolution via Temporal Distinction: Orientation Changes Based on Israel's Observance of Divine Will
Angled Position: Child Leaving Master Metaphor—Facing Bayit While Slightly Turned Toward Each Other

macro_segments.title: Access Rights to a Well Located Inside a Neighbor's House

micro_segments.title: Entry and Exit Hours: Access Only During Normal Times When People Ordinarily Enter and Exit
Restriction on Animal Access: Cannot Bring Animals; Must Draw Water Outside the House
Dual Locks: Owner and Well-Owner Each Hold Locks to Regulate Access and Protect Water
Explanation of Owner's Lock: Prevention of Unauthorized Entry to Protect Wife's Privacy (Chashad Ishto)

macro_segments.title: Access Rights to a Garden Located Behind a Neighbor's Garden

micro_segments.title: Entry and Exit Hours: Access Only During Normal Business Hours
Restriction on Commercial Use: Cannot Bring Merchants or Conduct Business Through the Path
Restriction on Shortcuts: Cannot Use Path as a Shortcut to Other Properties
Planting Rights: Outer Person Cannot Plant; Inner Person Can (Default Path Has No Exclusive Rights)
Side Path by Agreement: When Both Parties Agree to a Side Path, Unrestricted Access Hours and No Shortcut Restriction Remain

macro_segments.title: Irrigation Canal Rights: The Amah Bath Hashlachin and Parallel Scenarios

micro_segments.title: Sale of Irrigation Canal: Standard Allotments of Two Amot (or One Amah) for Expansion and Access
Amah Bath Hakillon (Non-Irrigation Use): Reduced Allotment of One Half-Amah on Each Side
Planting on the Edges: Owner's Right to Plant Trees vs. Prohibition on Vegetables (Chalchulei Mechanism)
Collapsed Banks: Right to Use Dirt from the Field for Reconstruction (Implicit Agreement)

macro_segments.title: Public Road Through Private Field: Transfer of Right and Its Validity

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Owner Relocates Public Road to Field Edge but Cannot Reclaim Original Path
Width Standards: Private Path (4 Amot) vs. Public Road (16 Amot) vs. King's Highway (No Limit)
Burial Road (Derech Hakever): No Limit on Width; Two Interpretations (Processional vs. Purchased Right)
Judicial Opinion from Tzipori: Space Required for Standing and Greeting Mourners

macro_segments.title: Analysis of the Public Road Transfer: Why the Transaction Cannot Be Undone

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda in the Name of Rav: The Transaction Is Illegitimate Due to Unilateral Authority
Rav Zevid in the Name of Rava: Enactment (Gezeirah) Against Twisted Roads (Derech Akalton) to Prevent Future Abuse
Rav Ashi's Principle: Any Alternative Path Is Twisted for Someone; Change Always Harms Segments of Population
Rabbi Eliezer's Principle (Tannaitic): Public Can Select a New Path If the Original Is Lost
Unresolved Question: Why Rabim Retain Both Old and New Paths Despite Illegitimate Transaction
